laws.title: Conditions Precedent to Retrenchment
laws.meaning: No workman can be retrenched unless given one month notice or pay in lieu, paid retrenchment compensation, and notified appropriate government authority.

laws.title: Responsibility for Payment of Wages
laws.meaning: Every employer is responsible for payment of wages to persons employed by them on or before the wage period deadline.
laws.punishment: Fine up to Rs. 7,500 for first offence and Rs. 22,500 for subsequent offences

laws.act: Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace Act, 2013
laws.title: Constitution of Internal Complaints Committee
laws.meaning: Every employer of a workplace must constitute an Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) to receive and redress complaints of sexual harassment.
laws.punishment: Fine up to Rs. 50,000 for non-constitution of ICC

laws.title: Power to Recover Damages
laws.meaning: Where an employer makes default in payment of contributions, the Central PF Commissioner may levy damages.
laws.punishment: Damages up to 25% of amount due

laws.title: Payment of Gratuity
laws.meaning: Gratuity is payable to an employee on termination of employment after 5 years of continuous service, at the rate of 15 days wages for each year of service.
laws.punishment: 6 months to 2 years imprisonment for non-payment

laws.title: Penalties for Offences
laws.meaning: Paying below minimum wages is punishable with imprisonment and fine.
laws.punishment: Up to 6 months imprisonment or Rs. 500 fine or both

questions.text: What is the nature of the labor dispute?
What type of employment did/do you have?
How long were you employed with this employer?
Do you have written employment documents?
Did you raise the grievance with HR or management?
What is the approximate amount in dispute (wages/dues)?

recommended_actions: Send legal notice to employer demanding dues payment within 15 days
File complaint with Labour Commissioner / Assistant Labour Commissioner
File complaint with POSH Internal Committee or Local Complaints Committee
Approach EPF Regional Office for PF default complaint
File claim with Controlling Authority under Payment of Gratuity Act
File application before Labour Court / Industrial Tribunal for reinstatement
Approach National Human Rights Commission if employer violates fundamental rights
